etimesDevotee watches Dhurandhar 2 night show and saves Rs 1,200 during Vaishno Devi yatra halt; here’s howA devotee's pilgrimage to Vaishno Devi took an unexpected turn when the yatra was paused. Facing an expensive hotel stay, Madhav found a clever solution: a 3-hour, 49-minute late-night screening of the popular film Dhurandhar 2. This provided comfortable accommodation for the night, saving him a significant amount and allowing him to proceed with his darshan plans the next morning.

etimesViral video claims Pakistan on ‘Indian spy alert’ after Dhurandhar 2 - here’s the truthFollowing the success of Ranveer Singh's film 'Dhurandhar 2', a viral video of Pakistani police conducting street checks was falsely linked to the movie. The footage, actually from an older operation in Karachi, sparked online speculation of a spy hunt. This incident highlights how easily the internet can misinterpret events, creating a fictional narrative around real-life occurrences.

etimes‘Better or worse isn’t the point’: Dutch mom breaks down 7 big parenting differences in India vs NetherlandsA Dutch mother in Mumbai shares her observations on parenting in India versus the Netherlands. She highlights the strong community support, children's integration into public life, and natural multilingualism in India. The article contrasts these with Dutch emphasis on early independence and play-based learning, noting the trade-offs and personal adjustments made.
